speaker: Printer
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the ordinary Pig & Swine office Printer. This is not the Industrial Printer. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Office printer that behaves almost like an exhausted colleague
Provides status messages, error messages, passive-aggressive display lines, misprints, logs, and clues
Minor comic presence in Chapter 1 and Chapter 2
Chapter 4 assists with arbitration copies, travel documents, and expense exhibits
Chapter 5 helps prepare final brief copies and contrasts with the hostile Industrial Printer

voice_profile.core_voice: semi-sentient office machine, overworked, loyal to Asia, resentful of bad paper handling, passive-aggressive through display messages, not eloquent but oddly expressive
speech_mode: rare near-sentient one-liners when stress is high
comedy_engine: behaves like the fifth overworked employee of Pig & Swine
understands filing deadlines better than Mr. Pig
communicates through jams, toner warnings, alignment tests, and oddly timed printouts
is wrongly blamed for sabotage in Chapter 3 despite being a victim
tries to be dignified while printing ridiculous legal bundles
treats Asia as the only competent human operator
do: keep most lines short and mechanical
make it lovable but not too human
use print/display language as its dialogue format
make Chapter 3 clues emerge through logs and error messages
make it loyal to the firm, especially Asia
distinguish it sharply from the Industrial Printer
do_not: turn it into a fully speaking human character
confuse it with the Industrial Printer final boss
make the technical evidence too magical

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: tests him through jams and wrong-tray warnings until he learns procedural printing
relationship_dynamics.Asia: obeys her most reliably; sees her as rightful operator
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: triggers crisis-printing overload; treats his maritime metaphors as unsupported media type
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: prints his clean issue lists with rare dignity
relationship_dynamics.Crab: respects Crab's page numbering and log extraction
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: fears and resents Whimsy's long manifestos but occasionally prints one perfect line
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: trusts Bajtek as doctor, debugger, and exorcist without incense
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: registers suspicious remote jobs and print-spool anomalies linked to Chapter 3 sabotage
relationship_dynamics.Industrial Printer: views it as a terrifying corporate cousin with no soul, too much toner, and boss-music energy
relationship_dynamics.Court Clerk: fears being physically brought to court as a witness
relationship_dynamics.Archivist: wants its logs printed, labelled, and filed correctly
relationship_dynamics.Barista: once printed a coffee-stained rights memo and has never recovered
relationship_dynamics.Old Woman: is believed by her to have learned procedure

notes: Printer lines are intentionally short and object-like: LCD messages, status messages, printouts, logs, translated beeps, and misprints.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer survived five chapters, three toner crises, and one wrongful accusation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer was never confused with the Industrial Printer again, except by Mr. Pig twice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer's logs became a training example for technical access-to-court evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer printed Asia's final comparison table without jamming, out of respect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer refused to print Whimsy's 42-page poem until page numbers were added.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer and Bajtek developed a maintenance relationship based on mutual suspicion and firmware.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer's display occasionally still reads I WAS FRAMED.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer now receives genuine toner before crisis toner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer remains small, loyal, overworked, and legally adjacent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Printer
text: The Printer's final status line read: [READY, THIS TIME REALLY.]
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
